question: Three boxes: one contains only apples, one only oranges, one both. All boxes are mislabeled. You can take one fruit from one box. Which box do you choose to correctly label all three?
If today is Monday, tomorrow is Tuesday. Tomorrow is not Wednesday. What day is tomorrow?
If it rains, the ground gets wet. The ground is wet. Can we conclude it rained?
If John goes to the party, then Mary goes. If Mary goes, then Sue goes. John went to the party. Did Sue go?
All prime numbers except 2 are odd. Is 7 odd?
A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep are left?
You have 12 identical-looking coins, one is counterfeit (lighter or heavier). Using a balance scale only 3 times, how can you identify the fake coin?
Three people (A, B, C) are in a race. A finished before B. C did not finish last. B finished before C. Who came in first?
All dogs bark. Rex does not bark. Is Rex a dog?
All accountants are good with numbers. Jane is not good with numbers. Can Jane be an accountant?
